_comment: Fuel and energy-storage properties for UAV propulsion sizing.
_sources: Roskam Vol. I §3.2 — propulsion selection and fuel types
ASTM D1655 — Aviation Turbine Fuels specification
NASA/CR-2011-217236 — Li-Po battery energy density survey

avgas_100ll.label: Avgas 100LL
avgas_100ll.notes: Low-lead aviation gasoline (ASTM D910). Standard fuel for piston UAV engines. Density 715–725 kg/m³ at 15°C.

jet_a.label: Jet-A / Jet-A1
jet_a.notes: Commercial aviation turbine fuel (ASTM D1655). Jet-A1 is the international variant with a lower freeze point (−47 °C vs −40 °C for Jet-A). Density 775–840 kg/m³ at 15°C.

jp8.label: JP-8 (Military Jet Fuel)
jp8.notes: NATO standard military turbine fuel (MIL-DTL-83133H). Broadly equivalent to Jet-A1 with added corrosion inhibitor, lubricity improver, and static dissipator. Density 775–840 kg/m³ at 15°C.

lipo_battery.label: Li-Po Battery Pack
lipo_battery.notes: Lithium-polymer cells including BMS, wiring, and structural enclosure. Volumetric density ~1 500–1 700 kg/m³ for packaged modules. Specific energy ~250 Wh/kg (0.9 MJ/kg). NOTE: the mission Breguet model assumes hydrocarbon fuel; electric range/endurance requires a separate e-propulsion module.

auto_selection._comment: Maps Drone.engine_type string to a fuel key when fuel_type='auto'.

_comment_ullage: 5 % of tank volume reserved for thermal expansion and vapour space (MIL-T-5578).
_comment_structure: 5 % added to geometric volume to account for tank wall thickness, baffles, and fittings. Total volume factor = 1 / (1 - ullage - structure) ≈ 1.11.
